extraction.one_line_summary: POLITT Nils won a sprint from a late breakaway group after crosswind chaos in the opening kilometers split the peloton but failed to produce sustained echelon warfare.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR isolated 40 riders back during early echelon formation but GC rivals failed to organize pressure, revealing passive tactics.
Deceuninck-QuickStep positioned CAVENDISH perfectly with ASGREEN second wheel immediately when crosswinds hit, demonstrating predictive wind positioning protocol.
After initial 20km chaos, peloton regrouped despite sustained crosswinds, suggesting teams prioritized sprint organization over opportunistic GC attacks.
LATOUR dropped immediately when echelons formed from right-to-left wind, confirming weak positioning anticipation by Total Energies in predictable crosswind sectors.

rider_signals.POLITT Nils: positive: won from breakaway after early echelon chaos, capitalizing on peloton's shift from GC opportunism to sprint control
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: positioned second wheel immediately with ASGREEN when crosswinds started, showing superior anticipation and team execution
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: caught 40 riders back in early echelons with few teammates but rivals failed to exploit vulnerability
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: second wheel positioning for CAVENDISH at echelon formation reveals designated wind-protection role in crosswind stages
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: negative: immediately dropped when echelons formed, exposing poor positioning instincts in predictable crosswind terrain
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: lost time at finish possibly by choice but showed nothing when attacked on previous stage, questioning form depth
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: rode Quintana off wheel on previous stage KOM, demonstrating superior climbing form retention
